subject: Computer Networking
title: DNS: Names to Numbers
description: A quick win: understand DNS: Names to Numbers.

hook.text: Humans remember names. Computers need numbers. DNS bridges the gap every time you visit a site.

buildup.text: DNS is hierarchical: root servers at the top, then TLD servers (.com, .org), then authoritative name servers.

discovery.text: A full lookup walks down the tree: root → .com → example.com → IP address. Each step narrows the search.

twist.text: Results are cached aggressively. Your device, router, and ISP all remember recent answers to save time.

climax.text: DNS was designed without encryption. DNS-over-HTTPS and DNS-over-TLS now protect your queries from snooping.

punchline.text: DNS translates names to IPs so you never have to.
